title: Small peripheral RUL tumor
ctFindings: Right upper lobe peripheral solid tumor measuring 2.4 cm.
No visceral pleural, chest wall, mediastinal, or diaphragmatic invasion.
No separate pulmonary nodules.
petFindings: FDG uptake limited to the primary tumor.
No suspicious nodal or distant uptake.
ebusFindings: No positive hilar or mediastinal nodes.
feedback: The primary tumor is greater than 2 cm and 3 cm or smaller, with no positive nodes or distant disease, so the stage group is IA3.

title: Reactive effusion with ipsilateral hilar disease
ctFindings: Left lower lobe tumor measuring 3.6 cm with visceral pleural contact.
Small pleural effusion is repeatedly cytology negative, non-bloody, and clinically favored reactive.
No pleural nodules.
petFindings: FDG-avid left hilar node.
No distant metastatic uptake.
feedback: The 3.6 cm tumor is T2a by size, 10L is ipsilateral hilar N1 disease, and the clinically reactive effusion is not counted as M1a.

title: Single-station N2
ctFindings: Right upper lobe tumor measuring 2.8 cm.
Single enlarged right lower paratracheal node.
petFindings: FDG uptake in the primary tumor and station 4R.
No distant metastatic uptake.
feedback: A right-sided tumor with one positive ipsilateral mediastinal station is N2a; T1cN2aM0 maps to stage IIB in the 9th edition.

title: Multi-station N2
ctFindings: Right lower lobe tumor measuring 4.2 cm.
Enlarged 4R and subcarinal lymph nodes.
petFindings: FDG uptake in the primary tumor, 4R, and station 7.
No distant metastatic uptake.
feedback: The 4.2 cm size is T2b and two N2 stations make this N2b; T2bN2bM0 maps to stage IIIB.

title: Chest wall invasion with one N2 station
ctFindings: Left upper lobe tumor measuring 6.2 cm with focal chest wall invasion.
Single suspicious 4L node.
petFindings: FDG uptake in the primary tumor and 4L.
No distant metastatic uptake.
feedback: Chest wall invasion and size both support T3, and a single ipsilateral mediastinal station is N2a; this combination maps to IIIA.

title: Small tumor, contralateral mediastinal node
ctFindings: Right upper lobe tumor measuring 2.1 cm.
Small but suspicious 4L node across the left lateral tracheal border.
petFindings: FDG uptake in the primary tumor and station 4L.
No distant metastatic uptake.
feedback: For a right-sided primary, 4L is contralateral mediastinal disease and therefore N3, even though the primary tumor itself is small.

title: Separate nodule in the same lobe
ctFindings: Right upper lobe primary tumor measuring 2.9 cm.
Second suspicious solid tumor nodule in the same right upper lobe.
No chest wall or mediastinal invasion.
petFindings: FDG uptake in both right upper lobe nodules.
No nodal or distant uptake.
feedback: A separate tumor nodule in the same lobe makes the tumor T3, even though the primary size alone would be T1c.

title: Separate nodule in another ipsilateral lobe
ctFindings: Left upper lobe tumor measuring 3.3 cm.
Separate suspicious nodule in the left lower lobe.
No pleural nodules or distant disease.
petFindings: FDG uptake in both left lung lesions.
No nodal or extrathoracic uptake.
feedback: A separate tumor nodule in a different ipsilateral lobe is T4, not M1a.

title: Malignant pleural effusion
ctFindings: Right upper lobe tumor measuring 1.8 cm.
Right pleural effusion with pleural nodularity.
petFindings: FDG uptake in the primary tumor and pleural nodules.
No extrathoracic metastatic uptake.
feedback: Malignant pleural effusion or pleural nodules are M1a, which makes the stage group IVA regardless of the small primary tumor.

title: Single adrenal metastasis
ctFindings: Left lower lobe tumor measuring 4.6 cm.
No separate lung nodules or pleural disease.
petFindings: FDG uptake in the primary tumor.
Single FDG-avid right adrenal lesion.
feedback: A single extrathoracic metastasis in one organ system is M1b and maps to stage IVA.

title: Multiple bone metastases
ctFindings: Right upper lobe tumor measuring 3.1 cm.
No pleural or pericardial nodularity.
petFindings: Multiple FDG-avid osseous lesions in the spine and pelvis.
No liver, adrenal, brain, or extrathoracic nodal metastasis.
feedback: Multiple bone metastases are multiple extrathoracic lesions in one organ system, so they are M1c1 and stage IVB.

title: Bone plus liver metastases
ctFindings: Left upper lobe tumor measuring 2.2 cm.
No pleural or pericardial disease.
petFindings: FDG-avid liver lesion and multiple FDG-avid osseous lesions.
No suspicious thoracic nodes.
feedback: Metastases across liver and bone involve multiple organ systems, so the M category is M1c2.
